desc: São Miguel is the Azores flagship island, combining volcanic lakes, geothermal valleys, coastal cliffs, and small city comforts around Ponta Delgada. Late spring to early autumn is the most popular weather window, while shoulder months can offer strong value with fewer crowds.
hls: Sete Cidades and Furnas volcanic landscapes
Thermal pools and geothermal cuisine traditions
Green crater lakes and dramatic coastline

todo.name: Sete Cidades viewpoints
todo.desc: Cover key rim viewpoints for the islands signature twin-lake panorama.

todo.name: Furnas geothermal valley
todo.desc: Combine hot springs, fumaroles, and traditional cozido cooking sites.

todo.name: Lagoa do Fogo hike
todo.desc: Take a trail day to one of the islands most photogenic crater-lake settings.

todo.name: Ponta Delgada old town
todo.desc: Explore compact historic streets, churches, and harbor-side plazas.

todo.name: Whale-watching trip
todo.desc: Book licensed marine operators in season for cetacean-spotting routes.

todo.name: Terra Nostra/thermal bath stop
todo.desc: Use geothermal pools for a recovery segment after hikes.

wifi.note: Reliable in Ponta Delgada and most hotels/apartments; rural areas can be less consistent.

prac.fltNote: No regular year-round nonstop Gatwick-São Miguel schedule; access is typically seasonal nonstop or one-stop via Lisbon/Porto. Typical returns are often £120-£260 off-peak and higher in summer peaks.
prac.visa: Portugal is in Schengen; UK and US passport holders can generally stay visa-free up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
alerts: Weather changes quickly; flexible plans and layers are essential.
Popular viewpoints can be fogged in—keep backup activity options.
Car hire is strongly recommended and can sell out in peak months.

sweet: May-June and September usually deliver the best weather/crowd balance across the island.
